items.title: Dozens of Aboriginal language custodians call for further revival efforts

children.content: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

gallery.altText: An over-the-shoulder shot of an Aboriginal child, blurred back of head in view, focus on hands holding a book.

thumbnail.altText: An over-the-shoulder shot of an Aboriginal child, blurred back of head in view, focus on hands holding a book.

items.title: Alleged Mongols associate arrested in rural Darwin after days-long manhunt

children.content: Northern Territory police officers have arrested Isaac Thompson, 26, in Darwin River, more than 48 hours after he escaped custody during a medical transfer.

image.altText: White man with dark brown hair, brown eyes, thick brown eyebrows with a moustache and beard.

items.title: NT minister claiming travel allowance when staying at property she owns

children.content: It comes after Jo Hersey's CLP colleague criticised Labor politician Manuel Brown for claiming the same allowance at times when staying at his Palmerston home.

image.altText: A female politician sitting down and smiling, inside a parliamentary chamber.

items.title: Single mother of six hit with $6k power bill after months of no communication

children.content: A single mother of six young children is paying off a $6,000 power bill after the Northern Territory's main energy retailer failed to send her a bill for almost 12 months. She's among thousands of Jacana Energy customers facing significant bills.

image.altText: A woman with straight brown shoulder length hair in a black tshirt, sitting at her kitchen table reading her bills.

items.title: Native title holders celebrate return of 'special country' in access win

children.content: Traditional owners from the Utopia region north of Alice Springs celebrate the recognition of their native title over an area encompassing more than 4,000 square kilometres.

image.altText: A federal court judge hands native title documents to native title holders.

items.title: A bus, a solar-powered stage and a journey across remote Australia

children.content: The Guts tour is a roaming, solar-powered convoy that snakes its way through some of the most remote communities in the Northern Territory, bringing together bands from the city and the bush.

articles.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

image.altText: podcast image NAIDOC week

articles.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

Person plays a drum kit as children gather around during a live music performance.
Tour bus drives along a remote dirt road beside a large termite mound.

items.title: Alice Springs tourism operators celebrate funding after toughest days

children.content: After several challenging years for tourism operators in central Australia, there is hope an incentive of free flights for visitors and a new grant fund will supercharge tourism in the region.

image.altText: A man and a woman standing together in warm clothing

items.title: Man, 34, in custody after girl, 17, found dead in remote NT home

children.content: Northern Territory Police say the girl was found inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, and was declared dead at the scene. A 34-year-old man known to the woman has been arrested.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Galiwin'ku on Elcho Island.

items.title: Remote NT MLA admits to claiming travel allowance during stays at city home

children.content: Manuel Brown's dumping from the role comes after weeks of scrutiny over his driving record and confirmation he allowed two of his underage children to drive his personal car on an outstation.

gallery.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

thumbnail.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

items.title: Telstra Triple Zero calls still impacted by 'secondary issue'

children.content: Telstra says work is ongoing to resolve a "secondary issue" impacting customers trying to make calls on its network, including to Triple Zero, but some people may still be unable to contact the emergency service.

articles.title: Victoria's regional rail network remains at standstill after Telstra outage

children.content: V/Line has advised commuters not to use its services, as a telecommunications outage continues to cause safety issues with the network.

image.altText: V Line trains

articles.title: Why a telco outage has stopped some trains but not others

children.content: Trains relying on the 4G network of freight rail track operator ARTC went down amid the Telstra failure.

image.altText: a regional train station with a line closure warning sign

articles.title: Telstra investigating secondary network fault affecting Triple Zero

children.content: Telstra is investigating a secondary network fault affecting Triple Zero callers since yesterday's outage. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The ASX stock market board showing lots red

articles.title: 'Shouldn't occur': Change demanded after Telstra outage

children.content: Advocates have demanded changes to how telecommunication companies are regulated and held accountable for outages after Telstra became the latest telco to suffer a large-scale network failure.

image.altText: A close up of a Telstra logo on the side of a pay phone

gallery.altText: A blurred woman with dark hair and a white top looking at a mobile phone in front of a clear Telstra orange and white logo.

thumbnail.altText: A blurred woman with dark hair and a white top looking at a mobile phone in front of a clear Telstra orange and white logo.

items.title: The UK's next PM has a carefully curated image. Not everyone is buying it

children.content: For some, Andy Burnham's casual communication style is refreshing. Others say it's a carefully crafted facade, disguising the fact that he's a ruthless Labour Party lifer.

image.altText: A man wearing a t-shirt and suit jacket, standing at a lecturn and smiling

items.title: Modi met with support and human rights protests as he arrives in Melbourne

children.content: A large swathe of the Indian diaspora wants to warmly welcome Narendra Modi to Melbourne, but others demand Australia put pressure on him over his human rights record.

image.altText: A man holds his hands together in a praying symbol as he looks at six people standing in a line.

items.title: Growers push to overturn ban on ancient 'superfood'

children.content: Farmers are appealing against a federal ban on the sale of
Moringa oleifera
as a food after a regulatory body determined there was insufficient evidence to prove its safety.

image.altText: A man holding up two pots in a greenhouse surrounded by small trees in pots.

items.title: Cost-of-living crisis 'hits from two directions' as major musicals close

children.content: As two more major musicals bite the dust, the local theatre industry hopes a live performance incentive modelled on a successful UK scheme will provide salvation.

image.altText: A man and a woman both dressed in blue sit beside each other on a park bench

items.title: As snowfall declines, a key ingredient is keeping the ski season alive

children.content: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

gallery.altText: Perisher June 29 shows not much snow on the slope.
Perisher July 1 shows barely any snow.

items.title: New South Wales writes a new fate in blood after epic decider

children.content: Against all odds and with most of their state doubting them, Laurie Daley's New South Wales side has pulled off one of the greatest wins in Blues history.

image.altText: A rugby league player in blue holds up his hands in celebration

items.title: 'Can't believe it': British wildcard books Wimbledon semifinal spot

children.content: After growing up just five minutes from Wimbledon, Arthur Fery is now through to the semifinals at his home grand slam.

image.altText: Arthur Fery doffs his cap to the crowd after his win

items.title: New varieties set Australian coffee industry up for expansion

children.content: Australians are among the world's biggest coffee drinkers but less than 1 per cent of the coffee consumed nationwide is grown locally. That could be about to change.

gallery.altText: Coffee pouring into shot glass from espresso machine

items.title: How First Nations voices enrich Australia’s classical music traditions

children.content: From James Henry to Nardi Simpson, Aaron Wyatt, Adam Manning and Nina Korbe, here are First Nations voices making waves in Australia’s classical music scene.

image.altText: Soprano Nina Korbe on stage with QSO in a blue dress and arms outstretched singing into the QPAC Concert Hall.

items.title: TV catchphrase quiz: Guess the show from the famous quote

children.content: Now for the story of 20 famous TV quotes, and the one quiz that had no choice but to keep them all together.

image.altText: Jessica Walter and Jason Bateman in a scene from Arrested Development.

items.title: Hobart 'Stadium 2.0' developer's company in liquidation with unpaid super, millions in debts

children.content: Tasmanian developer Dean Coleman's company, which was involved in the rejected 'Stadium 2.0' plan for Hobart, is in liquidation. He's now involved in a separate development proposal at Regatta Point.

items.title: Business fined for not allowing worker to leave site during meal breaks

children.content: An automotive industry group made threats "to create fear" in a man who had brought legal action against his employer for not giving him adequate lunch breaks, a tribunal judgement has found.

image.altText: close up of a person from side showing them biting into a ham and cheese toasted sandwich

items.title: Bridge upgrade turns 5-minute commute to 40 in Noosa tourist hotspot

children.content: Noosa locals are worried that businesses won't survive six months' worth of traffic diversions while a crucial bridge is upgraded.

image.altText: drone shot of the bridge

items.title: 'We'll sell his body parts': AI extortionists target missing man's parents

children.content: Scammers have sent the parents of a missing West Australian man an AI-generated image of their son and demanded thousands of dollars in exchange for his safety.

image.altText: A man with short dark hair and a close beard wearing a khaki green t-shirt sitting outside a playground.

items.title: Communities brace for job losses as SunRice winds back

children.content: Communities in southern NSW fear large cutbacks to rice processing could put hundreds of people out of work and decimate regional economies.

image.altText: A large industrial shed with two signs in front.

items.title: 'Profound injustice': Last woman executed in UK pardoned by king

children.content: The case of Ruth Ellis has gripped Britain for decades and inspired the 1985 film Dance with a Stranger.

items.title: Union says it raised safety concerns day before major train derailment

children.content: The rail union says it raised safety concerns with a freight train operator about 24 hours before a major derailment in the New South Wales Southern Highlands.

image.altText: emergency crew at scene of freight train derailment

items.title: Queensland records the most fatal motorcycle accidents in the country

children.content: Police say they've seen a rise in anti-social hooning behaviour as Queensland records a rise in fatal motorcycle crashes.

items.title: Aboriginal woman fights trespass charge, cites 'cultural obligation'

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

image.altText: People gathered outside court with an Aboriginal flag

items.title: ABC rejects claims of antisemitic bias ahead of fronting royal commission

children.content: The ABC and SBS will face questioning over complaints of bias and antisemitism when executives appear at the royal commission on Thursday.

image.altText: A sign shown on the outside of a large building.

items.title: High levels of PFAS in air force sparrows where new Melbourne suburb built

children.content: Blood samples found levels of the "forever chemicals" in sparrows near contaminated industrial and military sites were 10 times higher than those in a comparison population.

items.title: World Cup quarterfinals are upon us. Here's what you need to know

children.content: The World Cup has whittled 48 teams down to just eight, with the quarterfinals upon us and the finish line in sight. Find out all you need to know about these monumental matches.
